query: Coding task: implement a secure Sentinel Coder One command that tests Microsoft Foundry IQ grounding and reports sources to the user without leaking secrets.

**Chat visibility fixes:** latest assistant outpu"},{"ref_id":2,"title":"reports\training\SENTINEL_CODER_ONE_MICROSOFT_IQ_INTEGRATION.md chunk 1","terms":"reports\training\SENTINEL_CODER_ONE_MICROSOFT_IQ_INTEGRATION.md","content":"# Sentinel Coder Studio One — Microsoft IQ Integration **Date:** 2026-06-12 **Hackathon:** Microsoft Agents League Hackathon **Status:** IMPLEMENTED, COMPILED, PACKAGED, AND VERIFIED LOCALLY ## Required Microsoft IQ Layer Sentinel Coder Studio One now explicitly integrates a Microsoft IQ intelligence layer using: - **Primary layer:** Foundry IQ - **Purpose:** agentic knowledge retrieval / enterprise grounding before the coding agent answers - **Optional compatible modes:** Work IQ and Fabric IQ are exposed as selectable settings for future tenant-specific integrations ## Files Changed - [Microsoft IQ client](file:///D:/QubitDev/apps/qubitpage-aide/extensions/aide-agent/src/microsoftIq.ts) - [Chat panel integration](file:///D:/QubitDev/apps/qubitpage-aide/extensions/aide-agent/src/chatPanel.ts) - [VS Code settings contribution](file:///D:/QubitDev/apps/qubitpage-aide/extensions/aide-agent/package.json) - [Localized setting labels](file:///D:/QubitDev/apps/qubitpage-aide/extensions/aide-agent/package.nls.json) ## How It Works When `aide.microsoftIqEnabled` is enabled, Sentinel Coder queries the configured Microsoft IQ grounding endpoint before the model response. The returned grounding context is injected into the system prompt and the agent is instructed to cite retrieved sources. If the endpoint is missing or retrieval fails, Sentinel Coder reports that Microsoft IQ grounding was unavailable instead of pretending enterprise context was used. ## New Settings - `aide.microsoftIqEnabled` - `aide.microsoftIqLayer`: `foundry-iq`, `work-iq`, or `fabric-iq` - `aide.microsoftIqEndpoint` - `aide.microsoftIqApiKey` - `aide.microsoftIqTimeoutMs` - `aide.microsoftIqMaxContextChars` ## Secret Safety No API key is hardcoded. Keys can be read from VS Code settings or from environment variables: - `AZURE_FOUNDRY_IQ_API_KEY` - `MICROSOFT_IQ_API_KEY` ## Microsoft Documentation References - Microsoft IQ documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-iq/ - Foundry IQ knowledge retrieval for agents: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/agents/how-to/foundry-iq-connect - Foundry Agent file search/vector stores: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/agents/how-to/tools/file-search - Work IQ overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/copilot/extensibility/work-iq/ - Fabric IQ overview: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/iq/overview ## Hackathon Claim Sentinel Coder Studio One satisfies the Microsoft IQ re # Sentinel Coder Studio One — Microsoft IQ Integration **Date:** 2026-06-12 **Hackathon:** Microsoft Agents League Hackathon **Status:** IMPLEMENTED, COMPILED, PACKAGED, AND VERIFIED LOCALLY ## Required Microsoft IQ Layer Sentinel Coder Studio One now explicitly integrates a Microsoft IQ intelligence layer using: - With Foundry IQ, Sentinel grounds the answer in current Sentinel Coder One docs/release evidence, reports sources, and avoids inventing product-specific facts. ## Version 3.16.52 - Secure Foundry IQ runtime The Microsoft IQ runtime no longer spawns Azure CLI or shell commands from the VS Code extension. Foundry IQ Knowledge Base retrieve endpoints now require a bearer/JWT token supplied through `sentinelCoder.microsoftIq.apiKeyEnv` (default `MICROSOFT_IQ_BEARER_TOKEN`). Azure CLI is used only by local provisioning/test scripts, not by Marketplace runtime code. ## Version 3.16.51 - Real Foundry IQ Knowledge Base Retrieve Runtime Sentinel Coder One Studio now defaults to the verified Microsoft Foundry IQ Knowledge Base retrieve endpoint on `qubitpage-srch`: `sentinel-coder-iq-kb`. searchIndexArguments.search: Coding task: implement a secure Sentinel Coder One command that tests Microsoft Foundry IQ grounding and reports sources to the user without leaking secrets.

baseline.answer: Implement a command named `sentinel-coder.testFoundryIq`. Read the Foundry IQ settings, call the configured endpoint, and show a success or failure message. Keep credentials in environment variables, never print tokens, and log only status, source count, and latency. Add a small UI button that runs the command and displays PASS or FAIL.

grounded.answer:
Implement `sentinel-coder.testFoundryIq` as a production command grounded in Microsoft IQ / Foundry IQ.

1. Read settings from `sentinelCoder.microsoftIq.*`: enabled, layer, endpoint, apiKeyEnv, timeoutMs, and maxQueryChars.
2. Require `layer = foundry-iq` for the hackathon path.
3. Use a bearer token/API value from the configured environment variable only. Never write or echo the token.
4. POST a documented Knowledge Base retrieve request to the configured endpoint using `intents: [{ type: 'semantic', search: <query> }]` and `knowledgeSourceParams` for `sentinel-coder-iq-ks`.
5. Parse references/results into a compact grounding block: title, source, snippet/content preview.
6. Return a UI-safe result: PASS/FAIL, latency, source count, and redacted diagnostic message.
7. Add automated smoke coverage that verifies: endpoint reachable, retrieval returns sources, and the agent prompt contains the `Microsoft IQ / Foundry IQ grounding` section.
